behavior_summary: BONES::look: When a player/actor examines the bones item, the script marks the item/process for short-info handling and makes the item 'speak' its short description "skeleton " to the viewing process, then performs the engine's implies/pop/suspend handshake and returns.

BONES::use: When a player/actor uses the bones item the script checks an internal status word (Item::getStatus) and tests bit 0x0100. If that bit is clear it closes the item's UI/gump and returns. If the bit is set it performs a random roll (urandom(0x64)); on a rare outcome it has the item bark a warning "Do not defile my bones, live one!" and then suspends; otherwise it opens gump id 0x0C for the user.
notable_control_flow: Look path is linear: set info/process-exclude -> Item::bark("skeleton ") -> implies/pop/suspend -> return.
Use path branches on an item-status bit (0x0100). If clear -> Item::closeGump -> return (early exit). If set -> urandom(0x64) branch: rare bark + suspend -> return; else -> Item::openGump(0x0C).
The rare warning bark path includes an implies/pop/suspend sequence after Item::bark indicating the message is delivered then the process yields/suspends rather than opening UI.
open_questions: What precisely does the status bit 0x0100 represent on this bones item (locks, cursed state, protected state, or dialog-state)? — inferred but not explicit.
What exact probability threshold is intended by comparing urandom(0x64) to 0x5F (inferred ~4% if urandom returns 0..99, but exact urandom range/semantics are assumed).
What gameplay consequences (beyond message and suspend) result from the rare warning bark path (e.g., combat trigger, reputation change) — none observed in this script.
What exact engine semantics are performed by the implies/pop/suspend handshake used after bark (synchronization with the UI/engine vs. a process-yield) — inferred behavior only.

main_function.name: U8P_BONES::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player/actor 'U8P_BONES::look' interaction by presenting the item's short description to the viewer and performing the engine synchronization/return sequence.
entry_conditions: Event 0: actor/player examines (looks at) the BONES item
An interacting process id (actor PID) is passed to the handler (parameter at BP+06h)

function_map.name: U8P_BONES::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_BONES::look' (examine) interactions: set info/exclude and call Item::bark to show the short description.

function_map.name: U8P_BONES::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' interactions: test item status bit 0x0100, close gump if bit clear, otherwise either warn (rare) or open gump id 0x0C.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine to emit/display text from the item to a process/player.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Return the item's status/flags word used for bit testing.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Close the item's UI/gump for the interacting process.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Produce a pseudo-random integer used to implement a low-probability message branch.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Open a UI/gump (gump id 0x0C) for the interacting process.

external_calls.purpose: Show the item's short description "skeleton " to the viewer process (display text).
Read the item's status/flags to test bit 0x0100 and decide use behavior.
Close/cancel the item's UI when the status bit 0x0100 is clear (early return path).
Produce a random value (argument 0x64) to implement a small-probability branch for a warning message.
On a rare random outcome, have the item speak the warning "Do not defile my bones, live one!" to the user.
Open the item's UI/gump (id 0x0C) when status bit is set and the random check does not trigger the warning.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: BONES::look: When a player/actor examines the bones item, the script marks the item/process for short-info handling and makes the item 'speak' its short description "skeleton " to the viewing process, then performs the engine's implies/pop/suspend handshake and returns.
